item.title: Vector Search Engine Performance: 16x Faster Vamana Search
item.description: See how sembed-engine made Vamana vector search 16x faster with flat arrays, lightweight views, squared distances, SIMD-friendly loops, and cached candidate scores.
item.author: (Kartikay Dubey)

item.title: fast16 | Mystery ShadowBrokers Reference Reveals High-Precision Software Sabotage 5 Years Before Stuxnet
item.description: A previously unknown 2005 cyber sabotage framework patches high-precision calculation software in memory to silently corrupt results.

item.title: Why SWE-bench Verified no longer measures frontier coding capabilities
item.description: SWE-bench Verified is increasingly contaminated and mismeasures frontier coding progress. Our analysis shows flawed tests and training leakage. We recommend SWE-bench Pro.

item.description: AGENTS.md, skills, MCP, sandboxes, tests, and review agents are not random tricks.

item.title: Abort, Retry, No, Thanks – Unsung
item.description: A blog about software craft and quality

item.title: voice modems
item.description: If you've done much with modern cellphones, you've probably noticed just how odd the architecture can be around audio. Specifically, I mean call audio: modern smartphones have made call audio less of a special case (mostly by just becoming more complicated in general), but in older phones you would often find arrangements where the cellular modem 1 had direct analog audio to the microphone and speaker, perhaps via some switching to share amplifiers. That design meant that the cellular modem functioned basically as a completely independent device, a fully-capable "cellular phone" with the ability to make and receive voice calls. The role of the rest of the smartphone, and its operating system, was just to provide control messages for starting and ending calls.

item.title: DevOps Is a Culture, Not a Team: What I've Learned Building at Scale | Austin Xu
item.description: After 20 years running infrastructure at scale, here's what DevOps actually means — and why the most common implementation is an anti-pattern.
item.author: (https://github.com/austinxyz)

item.title: When the cheap one is the cool one
item.description: Apple and Porsche both figured out the same secret that the cheapest product in the lineup can be the most exciting. It just takes a little intention.

item.title: Welcome to the world of Statecharts
item.description: The world of statecharts describes what statecharts are, their benefits and drawbacks, how they differ from state machines, and practical examples on how to use them.

item.title: GoDaddy Gave a Domain to a Stranger Without Any Documentation
item.description: What would you do if your organization had used a domain name for 27 years, and the registrar holding the domain seized it without any advance warning? All
